codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of all 4 timeline-milestone visualizations (phase timeline, metric progression, threshold heatmap, retention overlay) in an 802-line charts.py module and 567 lines of app.py additions. All 29 new unit tests pass (115 total). The implementation covers all expectedBehavior items including: 9-phase timeline with click-to-filter, 3 metric progression charts with phase shading and discontinuity indicator, validation_failed red X markers, non-contiguous ID gap handling, threshold heatmap with blank old-era cells, and retention curve overlay with multi-select. A few non-blocking quality issues noted below.

issues.description: Unused variable `current_era` declared in `build_metric_progression`. Only `prev_exp_era` is used for contiguity checking. Dead code that could be removed for clarity.

`selected_phase` parameter accepted by `build_threshold_heatmap` but never used in the function body. The heatmap does not filter or highlight by phase. The parameter is harmless (caller always passes it) but misleading — a user reading the signature would expect phase filtering to affect the heatmap.

The `leaderboard_phase_filter` callback is a no-op — it always returns `dash.no_update`. Its docstring says 'Phase filter is handled in the leaderboard callback' but the main `update_leaderboard` callback (line 1121) does not read `selected-phase` as input. The actual phase filtering is done via the separate `update_leaderboard_by_phase` callback (line 1583) which writes to the same `leaderboard-grid` output with `allow_duplicate`. This works but is a fragile two-callback pattern — if callbacks execute in unexpected order, the leaderboard could briefly show unfiltered data.

`_phase_date_range` uses hardcoded date strings that won't update as the project evolves. These are approximate and documented as such, but could drift from reality. Not a functional issue since dates are only displayed in hover text.

When a non-validation_failed experiment has `val is None` (missing metric), it does NOT break the line segment — only the `prev_num_id` tracker advances. This means two scored experiments with an unscored experiment between them will have a continuous line connecting them, which could be misleading. The line visually implies interpolation over the missing data point. This is a minor UX concern since null-metric experiments are relatively rare.

The `update_leaderboard_by_phase` callback duplicates the filtering/sorting logic from `update_leaderboard`. If the filter logic changes in one place, it needs to be updated in both. Consider extracting shared filter+sort logic into a helper function.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill SKILL.md step 6 says 'For each verificationSteps item that starts with "agent-browser:", use the agent-browser skill to verify'. The worker was unable to verify Plotly chart click interactions and Dash 4.0 async dropdown interactions via agent-browser, as documented in skillFeedback.deviations. The skill should document this known limitation so future workers don't waste time attempting it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations: 'Could not fully automate Plotly chart click testing (phase timeline click-to-filter) or Dash 4.0 async dropdown interactions'. DiscoveredIssues also lists both as non_blocking.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The pattern of using `allow_duplicate=True` with `prevent_initial_call=True` for shared outputs across multiple callbacks is becoming common in app.py (at least 4 callbacks use this pattern). AGENTS.md doesn't mention this Dash 4.0 callback pattern, but it's essential for the phase filter → leaderboard integration and other cross-component updates.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: dashboard/app.py: lines 1004, 1318, 1404, 1583 — all use allow_duplicate=True for outputs that have multiple callbacks writing to them.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `typecheck` command in services.yaml only checks `app.py` and `data_loader.py` via py_compile, but doesn't include `charts.py`. The new charts.py module (802 lines) should also be type-checked.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml typecheck command: 'py_compile dashboard/app.py && py_compile dashboard/data_loader.py' — charts.py is missing. The `lint` command does check 'dashboard/*.py' though.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The leaderboard's `update_leaderboard` callback (line 1121) does not take `selected-phase` as input, so it's unaware of phase filtering. Instead, a separate `update_leaderboard_by_phase` callback (line 1583) overwrites the leaderboard data when phase changes. This dual-callback pattern for the same output is not documented as a convention in AGENTS.md and should be either documented or refactored to a single callback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: dashboard/app.py: update_leaderboard (line 1121) has no selected-phase input. update_leaderboard_by_phase (line 1583) writes to same 'leaderboard-grid' rowData with allow_duplicate=True.

summary: The phase-timeline-and-metric-progression feature is well-implemented with comprehensive coverage of all expectedBehavior items. The 802-line charts.py module provides clean, well-documented Plotly figure builders for all 4 visualizations, and the app.py integration adds proper navigation, callbacks, and era/phase filter propagation. All 115 tests pass (29 new). The implementation correctly handles: phase timeline with click-to-filter and toggle, 3 metric progression line charts with phase shading, Phase 4/5 scoring discontinuity indicator, validation_failed red X markers excluded from line interpolation, non-contiguous experiment ID gap handling, threshold heatmaps with blank old-era cells, and retention curve overlay with multi-select and max-5 enforcement. Minor issues: unused `selected_phase` param in heatmap builder, unused variable, no-op callback, and a dual-callback pattern for leaderboard phase filtering that could be fragile. None are blocking.
